title: alchemilla vulgaris var. longiuscula
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris var. longiuscula, commonly known as Lady's Mantle, is a perennial flowering plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North America, characterized by its rosy or white flowers and distinctive, velvety leaves. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, wounds, and mucous membranes. The medicinal properties of Lady's Mantle have been associated with various health benefits, including reduced bleeding, improved digestive health, and relief from menstrual cramps and menopausal symptoms. Lady's Mantle can be prepared as an herbal remedy in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
